name: ffdb

metadata.name: ffdb
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3

documentation:
# ffdb.py - Flat-File DB: nosql single file database and document storage

The module `ffdb` and the associated set of utility scripts
(`indexer`, `extractor`, `filestorer`, `remover` and `merger`)
allow the creation, maintenance and usage of a database and document storage
which employs a single file acting as a container for all the data.

This file can be distributed in several copies, made locally available or placed online
to be accessed remotely (e.g. over ftp or www).

The resulting database is hence accessible everywhere, without the need for any complex
installation and in particular without requiring any service to be running continuously.

* Simple 1-2-3 procedure:
1. Index your flat-file and/or choose documents to store
2. Place the resulting file wherever you want (locally or online, e.g. ftp or www)
3. Retrieve the entries or the documents from any device and location

**FFDB** can index entries according to any pattern, creating index of identifiers unique to each entry or shared across many. For a biological database this could mean for example to allow retrieval of all entries belonging to a certain species.

When requesting entries, the user can specify whether retrieving all the entries corresponding to the given identifier, or only the first or only the last one. This allows the possibility of storing a version history of the entries, continuously appending new entry versions to the file.

When indexing entries of a flat-file or storing documents inside the database,
the user can optionally specify to encrypt (AES) or compress (ZLIB) the entries/files.

Encrypted entries share a single master password but each has a unique IV ([Initialization vector](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Initialization_vector)). No plaintext information is sent or received, thus allowing secure access to encrypted entries of the database over insecure channels.

With an external utility ([gztool](https://github.com/circulosmeos/gztool)), the
container file can even be compressed as a whole, while still allowing retrieval of
single entries. This can be useful when said file needs to also be
distributed and employed in its entirety, without limiting its use only via `ffdb`.

If the container file is compressed with [bgzip](https://www.htslib.org/doc/bgzip.html),
then no external utility is needed.

## Documentation

* [GitHub page](https://github.com/g-insana/ffdb.py/)

## Download and installation

`ffdb` is pure python code. It has no platform-specific dependencies and should thus work on all platforms. It requires the packages `requests` `pycryptodomex` and `sortedcontainers`.
The latest version of `ffdb` can be installed by typing either:

``` bash
pip3 install -U ffdb
```
  (from [Python Package Index](https://pypi.org/project/ffdb/))

or:
``` bash
pip3 install git+git://github.com/g-insana/ffdb.py.git
```
  (from [GitHub](https://github.com/g-insana/ffdb.py/)).

The utility scripts should get installed for you by pip (if installed as --user you
may need to add e.g. ~/.local/bin/ to your $PATH).
Alternatively, you can directly download those you need:

``` bash
curl -LO https://github.com/g-insana/ffdb.py/raw/master/scripts/indexer.py
curl -LO https://github.com/g-insana/ffdb.py/raw/master/scripts/extractor.py
curl -LO https://github.com/g-insana/ffdb.py/raw/master/scripts/remover.py
curl -LO https://github.com/g-insana/ffdb.py/raw/master/scripts/merger.py
```

## Quickstart: some examples

``` bash
 #index a flat-file:

$ indexer.py -i '^name: (.+)$' 'email: (.+)$' -f addressbook >addressbook.idx

$ indexer.py -i '^AC   (.+?);' -f uniprot.dat -e '^//$' >uniprot.pac

 #store all the files contained in a directory:

$ filestorer.py -f myphotos.db -d iceland_photos/

 #add some more files

$ filestorer.py -f myphotos.db -s sunset.jpg icecream.jpg

 #extract a series of entries from the db

$ extractor.py -f uniprot.dat -i uniprot.pac -l sars_proteins

 #extract an entry from remote location

$ extractor.py -f http://remote.host/addressbook -i addressbook.idx -s john@abc.de

 #extract a file from the db

$ extractor.py -f myphotos.db -s sunset.jpg >sunset.jpg

 #merge two indexed flat-files

$ merger.py -f mydb -i mydb.idx -e newentries -n newentries.idx -d #(mydb will incorporate newentries)

 #remove a series of entries from the db

$ remover.py -f addressbook -i addressbook.idx -l disagreed_with_me.list
```

## Copyright

`ffdb` is licensed under the [GNU Affero General Public License](https://choosealicense.com/licenses/agpl-3.0/).

(c) Copyright [Giuseppe Insana](http://insana.net), 2020-

modules.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger
documentation:
merger.py
objective: merge new entries to existing flatfile, updating its index

by Giuseppe Insana, 2020
!python

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.check_index_compatibility
documentation: will check if old and new indexes are of same type

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.uncompress_file
documentation:
uncompress given filename
return name of the uncompressed file

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.compress_file
documentation:
compress given filename
optionally create gzindex
return name of the uncompressed file

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.concatenate_into
documentation:
first copy original file to a new file
then append content of addenda file at the end of the new file

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.append_to_buffered
documentation: append addenda file at the end of the given file, using buffered read/write

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.check_args
documentation: parse arguments and check for error conditions

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.check_iofiles
documentation: check for ability to open input/output filenames

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.merger.check_files
documentation: check for ability to open input/output filenames

modules.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover
documentation:
remover
objective: generalized entry remover from local flatfile, it deletes entries and reindexes

by Giuseppe Insana, 2020
!python

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.check_args
documentation: parse arguments and check for error conditions

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.check_files
documentation: do some checks on availability of resources

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.collect_entry
documentation:
find position and size of entries corresponding to specified identifier
from index

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.collect_entries_to_delete
documentation:
collect information about entries to delete, consulting the index,
return a sorted list and a dict

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.delete_entries
documentation:
delete the entries from input_filename writing into output_filename
according to given positions and sizes

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.find_shift_offset
documentation:
Find the offset to apply to a position,
depending on cumulative sizes of entries deleted before it

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.update_index_after_deletions
documentation:
update index file, creating outindex with entry positions shifted according to
information given

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.print_stats
documentation: print some final statistics on entries deleted

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.update_index_wrapper
documentation: wrapper to shift index for multithread use

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.init_thread
documentation: to initialise multithreaded worker

variables.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.remover.MINBLOCKSIZE
documentation: 40 kb of index identifiers per chunk

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.indexer.parse_ff_wrapper

modules.name: ffdb
documentation:
ffdb
This module and the associated set of utility scripts
allow the creation, maintenance and usage of a database and document storage
which employs a single container file.

by Giuseppe Insana, 2020
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: ffdb.calculate_chunknum
documentation:
calculate number of chunks from global defined blocksize
and file's size

functions.name: ffdb.calculate_blocksize
documentation:
calculate size of a single block
from number of chunk/splits desired and a file's filesize

functions.name: ffdb.find_next_delimiter
documentation: return the position of first delimiter found after given starting position

functions.name: ffdb.find_next_newline
documentation: return the position of first newline found after given starting position

functions.name: ffdb.chunk_startpos_onnewline
documentation: return the initial byte of a chunk without breaking newlines

functions.name: ffdb.chunk_of_lines
documentation: return a generator of the lines coming from a single chunk of the file

functions.name: ffdb.int_to_b64
documentation: convert a decimal integer to a base64encoded number

functions.name: ffdb.b64_to_int
documentation: convert a base64encoded number to decimal integer

functions.name: ffdb.format_indexes
documentation: return array of formatted indexes

functions.name: ffdb.deflate
documentation: compress a byte object

functions.name: ffdb.inflate
documentation: decompress a byte object

functions.name: ffdb.get_cipher_type
documentation: return cipher_type letter from cipher

functions.name: ffdb.find_keysize_cipher
documentation:
infer cipher and keysize from cipher_type letter
return keysize and cipher

functions.name: ffdb.merge_indexes
documentation:
merge two index files into one outfile, shifting the second one of given offset
returns number of identifiers from the second index

functions.name: ffdb.mergesort_file_array
documentation:
merge sort contents of a file and an array
writing to outputfile

functions.name: ffdb.heap_mergesort_file_array
documentation:
use heapq.merge to merge sort contents of a file and an array
writing to outputfile

functions.name: ffdb.mergesort_files
documentation:
use heapq.merge to merge sort contents of files
writing to outputfile

functions.name: ffdb.file_string_binary_search
documentation:
perform a binary_search for a string in a sorted text file
return all matches, unless mode specified (can be 'first' or 'last')

functions.name: ffdb._file_string_binary_search_any
documentation:
perform a binary_search for a string in a sorted text file
it will quickly return any match, not necessarily the first or the last in the file

functions.name: ffdb.get_position_first
documentation:
find from the index the position of the entry
corresponding to specified identifier

functions.name: ffdb.get_position_last
documentation:
find from the index the position of the entry
corresponding to specified identifier

functions.name: ffdb.get_positions
documentation:
find from the index all the positions
corresponding to (possibly duplicated) specified identifier

functions.name: ffdb.get_position_checksum_first
documentation:
find from the index the position of the entry
corresponding to specified identifier
additionally return checksum

functions.name: ffdb.get_position_checksum_last
documentation:
find from the index the position of the entry
corresponding to specified identifier
additionally return checksum

functions.name: ffdb.get_positions_checksums
documentation:
find from the index all the positions
corresponding to (possibly duplicated) specified identifier

functions.name: ffdb.init_cipher
documentation: initialize and return a new cipher

functions.name: ffdb.generate_iv
documentation: generate and return a new iv

functions.name: ffdb.derive_key
documentation: derive key of desired bytesize from passphrase

functions.name: ffdb.check_index
documentation:
read first record of a positional index and gather needed information
return index_type, cipher_name, keysize, has_checksums

functions.name: ffdb.read_from_size
documentation: reads from filehandle a specified amount of bytes

functions.name: ffdb.delete_files
documentation:
delete the temporary sub-files
optionally specify a path where to look for the files

functions.name: ffdb.delete_file_if_exists
documentation:
delete a file after confirming file exists
optionally specify a path where to look for the files

functions.name: ffdb.close_subfiles
documentation: close the filehandles for the temporary sub-files

functions.name: ffdb.print_subfiles
documentation:
read and print a series of temporary sub-files writing them sequentially
to a given output filehandle or print them to stdout

functions.name: ffdb.shift_index
documentation:
shift positions in index file by given offset
return list with identifiers/positions

functions.name: ffdb.shift_index_file
documentation: shift positions in index file by given offset creating outfile

functions.name: ffdb.encode_delimiter_re
documentation: encode a delimiter regexp, replacing any ^ and $ with newlines

functions.name: ffdb.postprocess_entry
documentation: perform any compression, encryption, checksum calculation as needed

functions.name: ffdb.entry_generator
documentation:
splits a file according to a delimiter, returning a generator of entries
terminating in the delimiter

functions.name: ffdb.compute_split_positions
documentation:
compute and return start positions and sizes of chunks of splitsize
without breaking lines or without breaking entries (if delimiter provided)

functions.name: ffdb.split_file
documentation:
split a file into chunks of splitsize without breaking lines
returns list of chunk filenames and list of the chunk filesizes

functions.name: ffdb.buffered_readwrite
documentation:
extract a piece of inputfile of given size from given position
and write it to outputfile

functions.name: ffdb.check_iofiles
documentation: check for ability to open input/output filenames

functions.name: ffdb.siprefix2num
documentation: Returns bytes from a metrix suffixed string

functions.name: ffdb.elapsed_time
documentation:
compute elapsed time from given start_time (in seconds) and return
well formatted string. If work_done argument given, compute speed of the process

functions.name: ffdb.eprint
documentation: print to stderr, useful for error messages and to not clobber stdout

variables.name: ffdb.REIV
documentation: only encrypted

variables.name: ffdb.REIVD
documentation: deflated and encrypted

variables.name: ffdb.REESIV
documentation: entrysize+iv stored

variables.name: ffdb.REESIVN
documentation: don't capture iv

variables.name: ffdb.REES
documentation: entrysize but no iv stored

modules.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor
documentation:
extractor
objective: generalized entry extractor from local/remote/compressed/encrypted flatfile

by Giuseppe Insana, 2020
!python

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.unzip
documentation: reverse of zip

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.batch
documentation: split iterable in constant-size chunks

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.find_chunkstart_size_common
documentation:
use u2c_map to calculate start and size of compressed blocks to fetch in order to
extract an entry given its uncompressed position and size
if cache used, prepare a filename into which caching needed blocks
for gztool:
    estimate how much needed to dl if cache not used

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gztool_init_new_zero_file
documentation: create temp_dl_file containing only chunkstart zeros (not taking space on disk)

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gztool_retrieve_and_append_gzchunk
documentation:
extend given chunk retrieving remote compressed data and appending to it
optionally rename it to newname to store in cache

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gztool_retrieve_and_prepend_gzchunk
documentation:
extend given chunk retrieving remote compressed data and prepending to it
optionally rename it to newname to store in cache

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gzchunks_merge_adjacent_common
documentation:
merge adjacent gzchunks into new file, removing the two which got merged
e.g. merging 1-1 with 2-2

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gzchunks_merge_overlapping_common
documentation:
merge overlapping gzchunks into new file, removing the two which got merged
e.g. merging 0-3 with 3-4

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.tail_extend_gzchunk_common
ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.head_extend_gzchunk_common
ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.retrieve_cached_chunk_common
ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.find_best_cached_chunk
documentation:
look among already downloaded chunks whether there is one which
totally or partially contains the data we need

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.retrieve_from_size_and_save_bgzip
documentation:
reads from remote url a specified amount of bytes and save them to a file
optionally move to a new file once download is completed

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.retrieve_from_size_and_save_gztool
documentation:
reads from remote url a specified amount of bytes and save them to a file
after first init with zeros the file
optionally move to a new file once download is completed

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.retrieve_from_size
documentation: reads from remote url a specified amount of bytes

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.get_remote_size
documentation: fetches and returns size of a remote url

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.chunkdist
documentation:
given two segments defined as 'STARTPOS-ENDPOS' return their distance
0 dist means adjacent; negative indicates overlap

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.chunk_dist_from
documentation:
given first segments defined as STARTPOS, ENDPOS
and second segment defined as 'STARTPOS-ENDPOS'
return their distance
0 dist means adjacent; negative indicates overlap
used as sorting function in retrieve_cached_chunk_bgzip()

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.cache_cleanup
documentation: house-cleaning and reorganization of compressed remote files cache

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.find_compressratio
documentation: find how much data was compressed

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.print_u2c_map_gztool
documentation: print uncompressed to compressed index mapping (debug)

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.print_u2c_map_bgzip
documentation: print uncompressed to compressed index mapping (debug)

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.build_u2c_map_gztool
documentation: to build uncompressed to compressed index mapping for gztool index

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.build_u2c_map_bgzip
documentation:
to build uncompressed to compressed index mapping for bgzip index
bgzip .gzi index format:
        uint64_t number_entries
    followed by number_entries pairs of:
        uint64_t compressed_offset
        uint64_t uncompressed_offset

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gz_block_startpos_gztool
documentation: start position (in compressed bytes) of a given gztool blockid

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gz_block_startpos_bgzip
documentation: start position (in compressed bytes) of a given bgzf blockid

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.gz_block_endpos
documentation: end position (in compressed bytes) of a given compressed file blockid

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.u2b_gztool
documentation: return the gztool blockid holding the position corresponding to the uncompressed position given

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.u2b_bgzip
documentation: return the bgzf blockid holding the position corresponding to the uncompressed position given

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.check_args
documentation: parse arguments and check for error conditions

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.check_files
documentation: do some checks on availability of resources

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.print_entry
documentation: apply eventual post-processing and then print entry to filehandle

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.index_binary_search
documentation: perform a binary search inside the gzindex of compressed/uncompressed points

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.fetch_remote_compressed_entry_gztool
documentation: fetch compressed chunk from url and optionally store it in cache

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.fetch_remote_compressed_entry_bgzip
documentation: fetch compressed chunk from url and optionally store it in cache

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.fetch_compressed_entry_gztool
documentation:
use gztool to extract a gzip compressed_entry
from given position of specified flatfile

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.fetch_compressed_entry_bgzip
documentation:
extract a bgzip compressed_entry
from given position of specified flatfile

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.collect_index
documentation:
find and decode position of entries corresponding to specified identifier from index
return zipped arrays of decoded positions, sizes and optionally iv and checksums

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.merge_adjacent
documentation:
find if adjacent entries requested and group them together before extraction
strategy:
check whether next entry follows previous.
 if yes: add to a merged entry with same position, sum of entry_sizes,
 collation of remaining data and keeping original entries' size and pos for
 later unpacking by print_entry
 otherwise: keep as normal (isolated) entry

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.extract_entry
documentation:
find position of entry corresponding to specified identifier from index
calls retrieve_entry (which calls print_entry)

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.retrieve_entry
documentation:
Note: for remote flatfiles, flatfilefh is ignored, using instead args.flatfile (the url)
retrieve the entry from the flatfile
calls print_entry

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.collect_extract_entries
documentation:
identify entries to extract and collect them, optionally merge together, then extract them
either single or multithreaded

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.retrieve_entries
documentation: use the (now merged where possible) indexes to retrieve entries from flatfile

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.extract_entries
documentation: identify entries to extract and extract them

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.print_stats
documentation: if verbose print some final statistics on entries extracted (or failed to extraxct)

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.init_thread_blockmode
documentation: to initialise multithreaded worker

functions.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.init_thread_collectmode
documentation:
to initialise multithreaded worker after indexes already collected
for merged_adjacent strategy

variables.name: ffdb-2.5.6.data.scripts.extractor.MINBLOCKSIZE
documentation: 10 kb of identifiers list per chunk
